cfad500210b7c17496294607654377aa346559f6.title: Light-Cell Polynomial Rate
cfad500210b7c17496294607654377aa346559f6.body:
Fix an overlap constant \(\epsilon\) with \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\). The hybrid estimator is computable in the real-arithmetic model: there is a universal operation-count constant \(K>0\) such that, for every \(n\) and \(d\), a real-arithmetic program reading the hybrid count vector returns \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) exactly and uses at most \(K d M(n)^4\) arithmetic and comparison operations.

Moreover, there exist constants \(C_\epsilon,c_\epsilon>0\), depending only on \(\epsilon\), such that for every sample size \(n\), alphabet size \(d\), law \(P\) on \(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,1\}\), and sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}, if
\[
d\le c_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then the light-cell contribution satisfies
\[
E_{P^{\otimes n}}\left[\left\{\widehat T_{\mathcal L}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\phi(q_k)\right\}^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

a1e2b7df6dc136b12e8a9e2545126a65f242811c.title: Pilot Sandwich Bound
a1e2b7df6dc136b12e8a9e2545126a65f242811c.body:
Let \(L=\log(en)\). For a sample satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling} with single-observation law \(P\), let \(\widehat{\mathcal H}\) be the pilot heavy set at threshold \(t\), let \(\widehat{\mathcal L}=\widehat{\mathcal H}^{c}\), and write \(m_0=|I_0|\), \(B_-=tL/(2m_0)\), and \(B_+=2tL/m_0\).

For every \(K>0\) and \(c>0\), there exist constants \(t_0>0\), \(C>0\), and an integer cutoff \(N\) such that, for every \(t\ge t_0\), every \(n\ge N\), every dimension \(d\), every law \(P\) on the finite observation alphabet, and every iid sample law satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling}, if
\[
d\le c\,n\,L,
\]
then
\[
\Pr\!\left(\widehat{\mathcal H}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\ge B_-\}\ \text{or}\ \widehat{\mathcal L}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\le B_+\}\right)
\le C n^{-K}.
\]
Moreover, for every \(c>0\), there exist \(C>0\) and an integer cutoff \(N\) such that the same conclusion holds for all \(n\ge N\), \(d\le c\,n\,L\), laws \(P\), and iid sample laws satisfying Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling}, with \(t=256\) and \(K=4\):
\[
\Pr\!\left(\widehat{\mathcal H}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\ge 256L/(2m_0)\}\ \text{or}\ \widehat{\mathcal L}\not\subseteq\{k:p_k\le 512L/m_0\}\right)
\le C n^{-4}.
\]

1fe65ba80877b00c59e33c3d95ed1400a51a706a.title: Heavy Cell Rate
1fe65ba80877b00c59e33c3d95ed1400a51a706a.body:
For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon>0\) and an integer \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that the following holds.  For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), every single-observation law \(P\) on \(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}\times\{0,1\}\), and every sample law \(\mu_n\), assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} \(n\ge N_\epsilon\).
\item \textbf{(Dimension growth.)} \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\).
\item \textbf{(Experiment class.)} \(\mu_n=P^{\otimes n}\) belongs to \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) in the sense of Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\int \left(\widehat T_{\mathcal H}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\phi(q_k)\right)^2\,d\mu_n
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

153592f6818e5a6b83530069ff7a7260855d7c5b.title: Two-Category Witness
153592f6818e5a6b83530069ff7a7260855d7c5b.body:
For every \(\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) such that \(0<\epsilon\) and \(\epsilon\le 1/2\), let \(Q\) be the full-data law constructed in Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-category-witness}, and let \(P\) be its observed marginal law. Then \(Q\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:consistency} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:conditional-exchangeability}, the observed law \(P\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap} with overlap constant \(\epsilon\), and the ATE functional of Definition~\ref{obj:def:ate-functional} obeys
\[
\tau(P)=\frac12,
\qquad
E_P[Y\mid A=1]-E_P[Y\mid A=0]=\epsilon .
\]

4b9d426114194932321289794334941234d5ec64.title: Bernoulli Randomization Lower Bound
4b9d426114194932321289794334941234d5ec64.body:
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the minimax mean-squared-error risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} satisfies
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\ge \frac{1}{100n}.
\]

433e41d4fd9a3a9d7c1a71a204125e827cce7719.title: Randomized Endpoint Minimax Risk
433e41d4fd9a3a9d7c1a71a204125e827cce7719.body:
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the minimax mean-squared-error risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} at exact randomization satisfies
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac{1}{n}.
\]

67b2bfaa03a1f06ab2b2a4f6da70aa8c90380fe7.title: ATE Minimax Lower Bound
67b2bfaa03a1f06ab2b2a4f6da70aa8c90380fe7.body:
For any \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), let \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) denote the minimax mean-squared-error risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}. There exist constants \(a_\epsilon,b_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and a natural-number cutoff \(N_\epsilon\) such that \(a_\epsilon>0\), \(b_\epsilon>0\), and for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le b_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
one has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\ge
a_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]

1679ddb2202d05acc32fd02d1a63126725df5240.title: Centered Linear MSE Bound
1679ddb2202d05acc32fd02d1a63126725df5240.body:
For any \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), any \(\epsilon\in\mathbb R\), any single-observation law \(P\), and any sample law \(\mu_n\), suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} \(n>0\).
\item \textbf{(Experiment class.)} \(\mu_n\) belongs to the experiment class \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) generated by \(P\), as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}.
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}
=
\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n
2(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right).
\]
Then, with \(\tau(P)\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:ate-functional},
\[
\mathbb E_{\mu_n}\left[\left(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4\left(\frac12-\epsilon\right)^2.
\]

70f1bb60095fe3d35d224fe517589e3ea5cfcaba.title: Combined Upper Envelope
70f1bb60095fe3d35d224fe517589e3ea5cfcaba.body:
Fix constants \(\epsilon,C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Interior overlap.)} \(0<\epsilon<1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Positive constants.)} \(C_\epsilon>0\) and \(\rho_\epsilon>0\).
\item \textbf{(Hybrid bound.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), if \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), then the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\end{itemize}
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), let \(K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}\), and define the selected estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{sel}}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. Then, with \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk},
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
K_\epsilon\left[
\frac1n+
\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}
\right].
\]

aaec50055e1d29bad26cf874a9054fdac7874c9d.title: Universal Hybrid Calibration
aaec50055e1d29bad26cf874a9054fdac7874c9d.body:
The following conclusions hold for the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) and centered estimator \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle}, with minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Computability.)} There is a constant \(K\in\mathbb N\), \(K>0\), such that for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) there is a real-arithmetic program which, on the hybrid count input, returns exactly \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) for every sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\), and whose arithmetic/comparison operation count is at most \(K\,d\,M(n)^4\).

\item \textbf{(Fixed-overlap hybrid calibration and envelope.)} For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon>0\), \(\rho_\epsilon>0\), and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\) such that, for all \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\), \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
the hybrid estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
For the deterministic selector which chooses \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and otherwise chooses \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\), one also has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\]
and
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}
\left\{
\frac1n+
\min\left[
\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\right]
\right\}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Centered estimator.)} For every \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\) and every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2 .
\]

\item \textbf{(Endpoint bracket.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac1n .
\]
\end{itemize}

4c3a59b595f8f68c3bed4a663249a3fbd1203f06.title: Fixed-Interior Minimax Rate
4c3a59b595f8f68c3bed4a663249a3fbd1203f06.body:
Fix \(0<\epsilon<1/2\). Then there exist constants \(a_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon,C_\epsilon>0\) and an integer \(N_\epsilon<\infty\) such that the following hold.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sharp sandwich.)} For every \(n,d\), every single-observation law \(P\), and every sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:experiment-class}, if \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then, with \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle},
\[
a_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{Q^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_Q\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(Q)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Parametric window.)} For every \(K>0\), there exists \(C_K>0\) such that, for every \(n,d\), every single-observation law \(P\), and every sample law \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\), if \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\),
\[
d\le K\sqrt n\,\log n
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
then
\[
\frac{a_\epsilon}{n}
\le
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\frac{C_K}{n}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Consistency threshold.)} For any sequences \(n_j,d_j\) with \(n_j\to\infty\), eventually \(d_j>0\), and eventually
\[
d_j\le \rho_\epsilon n_j\log n_j,
\]
one has
\[
\mathsf R_{n_j,d_j,\epsilon}\to0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{d_j}{n_j\log n_j}\to0.
\]
\end{itemize}

29885fe8fe3949061912d03bbeec43c6e642320f.title: Iid sampling
29885fe8fe3949061912d03bbeec43c6e642320f.body: The observations \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) are independent and identically distributed with single-observation law \(P\).

2fff01bdfaeb31c530e23535bbeb20dcbf9c2718.title: Consistency
2fff01bdfaeb31c530e23535bbeb20dcbf9c2718.body:
The observed outcome satisfies
\[
Y=Y(A)
\]
almost surely.

e3aa1338e1b91e9cf101ce1cd2a2b079fed32d6b.title: Conditional exchangeability
e3aa1338e1b91e9cf101ce1cd2a2b079fed32d6b.body:
The binary potential outcomes satisfy
\[
(Y(0),Y(1))\perp A\mid X .
\]

b30cc84f5d6a7e3caef6d4220234dae5b4a36379.title: Experiment class \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\)
b30cc84f5d6a7e3caef6d4220234dae5b4a36379.body:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap}, the experiment class is
\[
\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}
:=
\{P^{\otimes n}: P\in\mathsf{Prob}(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}^2),\ n,d\in\mathbb N,\ 0<\epsilon\le 1/2,\ P\text{ satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap}}\}.
\]

2b22af863626b63ce68133eb9783b331634a064a.title: Overlap cone \(\mathcal C_\epsilon\)
2b22af863626b63ce68133eb9783b331634a064a.body:
The overlap-restricted cone of nonnegative four-cell vectors is
\[
\mathcal C_\epsilon
:=
\{u=(u_{00},u_{01},u_{10},u_{11})\in\mathbb R_+^4:
\epsilon(u_{00}+u_{01}+u_{10}+u_{11})
\le u_{10}+u_{11}
\le (1-\epsilon)(u_{00}+u_{01}+u_{10}+u_{11})\}.
\]

8c81e758530120233a68d4c478994dc286c40a5d.title: Cell functional \(\phi(u)\)

f306aa37c36b4f09464eaeb139df967e02a5f431.title: ATE functional \(\tau(P)\)
f306aa37c36b4f09464eaeb139df967e02a5f431.body:
The average treatment effect functional is
\[
\tau(P)
=
\sum_{k=1}^{d}p_k(\mu_{1k}-\mu_{0k})
=
\sum_{k=1}^{d}\phi(q_k).
\]

b51940b76b22d6b5cae856d3a21fdda9292409cf.title: Hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\)
b51940b76b22d6b5cae856d3a21fdda9292409cf.body:
Set
\[
I_0=\{1,\ldots,\lfloor n/2\rfloor\},\qquad
I_1=\{\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1,\ldots,n\},\qquad
m_j=|I_j|,\qquad
L=\log(en).
\]
Let \(A=6\), and set
\[
\lambda_0=256,\qquad
b_0=4096,\qquad
D_A=8\log(9A/8)=8\log(27/4),
\]
and
\[
\alpha_0
=
\min\left\{
1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}
\right\}.
\]
For \(n\ge2\), define
\[
M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\},
\qquad
B(n)=b_0L/m_1.
\]
Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\),
\[
\alpha_0L\ge2,\qquad
4M(n)^2\le m_1,\qquad
4M(n)/m_1\le 3B(n)/4.
\]
For \(n\ge N_0\), write \(M=M(n)\) and \(B=B(n)\), and define the pilot-certified heavy and light category sets by
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n
=
\left\{
k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L
\right\},
\qquad
\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
For the second split, put
\[
N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky},
\qquad
N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky},
\]
and define the heavy-category ratio contribution
\[
\widehat h_k
=
\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}
\left\{
\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}
-
\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}
\right\}.
\]
Let
\[
H_M(x)=\frac{1-T_M(1-2x)}{2M^2},
\qquad
E_M(x)=\frac{H_M(x)}{x},
\qquad
G_M(x)=\frac{1-E_M(x)}{x},
\]
where \(E_M\) and \(G_M\) are interpreted by polynomial continuation at zero. For \(u=(u_{00},u_{01},u_{10},u_{11})\), set
\[
s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1},\qquad
s(u)=s_0(u)+s_1(u),\qquad
t_1(u)=u_{11},\qquad
t_0(u)=u_{01}.
\]
Define the light-cell polynomial approximation
\[
P_{M,B}(u)
=
\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B
G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)
-
\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B
G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right).
\]
Expanding \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), set
\[
\widehat P_k
=
\sum_{|r|\le M}
c_r
\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},
\qquad
\widehat T_{\mathcal H}
=
\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,
\qquad
\widehat T_{\mathcal L}
=
\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k.
\]
The truncated balanced ratio-polynomial hybrid estimator is
\[
\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}
=
\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}.
\]
For \(n<N_0\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The estimator uses no overlap constant and is computable in \(O(dM^4)\) arithmetic operations.

343c270e6c24b1a84b3b2153c76d6faa05cf0e99.title: Minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\)

4fd7bca745d387c62c990b48c8ad1416677d54f0.title: Two-category witness law
4fd7bca745d387c62c990b48c8ad1416677d54f0.body:
For \(d=2\), set
\[
p_1=p_2=\frac12,\qquad
\pi_1=\epsilon,\qquad
\pi_2=1-\epsilon,\qquad
\mu_{01}=\mu_{02}=0,\qquad
\mu_{11}=1,\qquad
\mu_{12}=0.
\]
Define a full-data extension by drawing
\[
(Y(0),Y(1))=(0,\mathbf 1\{X=1\}),
\]
and then drawing \(A\mid X=k\sim\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\).

1dc50564c7b6c49c329cce22b3727a24ce09727a.title: Two-Category Confounding Witness
1dc50564c7b6c49c329cce22b3727a24ce09727a.body:
For \(\epsilon\), suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Real parameter.)} \(\epsilon\in\mathbb R\).
\item \textbf{(Positive contrast.)} \(0<\epsilon\).
\item \textbf{(Overlap range.)} \(\epsilon\le 1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Witness law.)} \(Q\) is the full-data law constructed in Definition~\ref{obj:def:two-category-witness}, and \(P\) is its observed marginal law.
\end{itemize}
Then \(Q\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:consistency} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:conditional-exchangeability}, the observed law \(P\) satisfies Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:overlap} with overlap constant \(\epsilon\), and the ATE functional of Definition~\ref{obj:def:ate-functional} obeys
\[
\tau(P)=\frac12,
\qquad
E_P[Y\mid A=1]-E_P[Y\mid A=0]=\epsilon .
\]

893f616b67992c6267bdc48b48d552026040fb9b.title: Pilot Sandwich Bound

6f796af9999dd463524336b9e8279792b0a59055.title: Hybrid Calibration And Endpoint
6f796af9999dd463524336b9e8279792b0a59055.body:
The following conclusions hold for the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} and the minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Computability.)} There is a constant \(K\in\mathbb N\), \(K>0\), such that for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) there is a real-arithmetic program which, on the hybrid count input, returns exactly \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) for every sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\), and whose arithmetic/comparison operation count is at most \(K\,d\,M(n)^4\).

\item \textbf{(Fixed-overlap hybrid calibration and envelope.)} For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon>0\), \(\rho_\epsilon>0\), and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\) such that, for all \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\), \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
the hybrid estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
For the deterministic estimator that chooses \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and otherwise chooses \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\), one also has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\]
and
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}
\left\{
\frac1n+
\min\left[
\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\right]
\right\}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Centered estimator.)} For every \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\) and every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the centered estimator
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}
=
\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n
2(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2 .
\]

\item \textbf{(Endpoint bracket.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac1n .
\]
\end{itemize}

55870b9b752f18d401a52ec00d9511b1b8776824.title: Universal Hybrid Envelope
55870b9b752f18d401a52ec00d9511b1b8776824.body:
For the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} and the minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}, the following conclusions hold.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Computability.)} There is a constant \(K\in\mathbb N\), \(K>0\), such that for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) there is a real-arithmetic program which reads the hybrid count input, returns exactly \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) for every sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\), and has arithmetic/comparison operation count at most \(K\,d\,M(n)^4\). The hybrid estimator itself does not take \(\epsilon\) as an input.

\item \textbf{(Fixed-overlap hybrid calibration and selected envelope.)} For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon>0\), \(\rho_\epsilon>0\), and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\) such that, for all \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\), \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
the hybrid estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
Define \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{sel}}(C_\epsilon,\epsilon)\) to be the deterministic estimator that equals \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and equals \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. Then
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{sel}}(C_\epsilon,\epsilon)-\tau(P))^2\right],
\]
and
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{sel}}(C_\epsilon,\epsilon)-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}
\left\{
\frac1n+
\min\left[
\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\right]
\right\}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Centered estimator.)} For every \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\) and every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\), the centered estimator
\[
\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}
=
\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n
2(2A_i-1)\left(Y_i-\frac12\right)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2 .
\]

\item \textbf{(Endpoint bracket.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac1n .
\]
\end{itemize}

c5b92d80d3590743a6dabd0079cb95168382eaea.title: Universal Hybrid Envelope
c5b92d80d3590743a6dabd0079cb95168382eaea.body:
The following conclusions hold for the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) and centered estimator \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle}, with minimax risk \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Computability.)} There is a constant \(K\in\mathbb N\), \(K>0\), such that for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) there is a real-arithmetic program which, on the hybrid count input, returns exactly \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) for every sample \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\), and whose arithmetic/comparison operation count is at most \(K\,d\,M(n)^4\).

\item \textbf{(Fixed-overlap hybrid calibration and envelope.)} For every \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), there exist constants \(C_\epsilon>0\), \(\rho_\epsilon>0\), and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\) such that, for all \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\), \(d>0\), \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), and
\[
d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n,
\]
the hybrid estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
For the deterministic selected estimator obtained from the rule that chooses \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and otherwise chooses \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\), one also has
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\]
and
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}
\left\{
\frac1n+
\min\left[
\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\right]
\right\}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Centered estimator.)} For every \(0<\epsilon\le 1/2\) and every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
E_P\!\left[(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}-\tau(P))^2\right]
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2 .
\]

\item \textbf{(Endpoint bracket.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(n>0\) and \(d>0\),
\[
\frac{1}{100n}\le \mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le \frac1n .
\]
\end{itemize}

b6ab064eb6f9c459153c9a8d5ca4be6e131fb10b.title: Combined Upper Envelope
b6ab064eb6f9c459153c9a8d5ca4be6e131fb10b.body:
Fix constants \(\epsilon,C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Interior overlap.)} \(0<\epsilon<1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Positive constants.)} \(C_\epsilon>0\) and \(\rho_\epsilon>0\).
\item \textbf{(Hybrid bound.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), if \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), then the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\end{itemize}
For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), let \(K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}\). Define \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. Then, with \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk},
\[
\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}
\le
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{sel}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
K_\epsilon\left[
\frac1n+
\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}
\right].
\]

f2898bd0aafdb263dfcabc29477bb463081e3c49.title: Combined Upper Envelope
f2898bd0aafdb263dfcabc29477bb463081e3c49.body:
Fix constants \(\epsilon,C_\epsilon,\rho_\epsilon\in\mathbb R\) and \(N_\epsilon\in\mathbb N\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Interior overlap.)} \(0<\epsilon<1/2\).
\item \textbf{(Positive constants.)} \(C_\epsilon>0\) and \(\rho_\epsilon>0\).
\item \textbf{(Hybrid bound.)} For every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\), if \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), then the hybrid estimator \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:hybrid-estimator-handle} satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}.
\]
\end{itemize}
Then for every \(n,d\in\mathbb N\) with \(0<n\), \(N_\epsilon\le n\), and \(d\le \rho_\epsilon n\log n\), let \(K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}\). Define the \(C_\epsilon,\epsilon\)-dependent selected estimator \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2}\right\}
\le
\frac1n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise. This selected estimator satisfies
\[
\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}
\mathbb E_{P^{\otimes n}}\!\left[\left(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}-\tau(P)\right)^2\right]
\le
K_\epsilon\left[
\frac1n+
\min\left\{\frac{d^2}{n^2(\log n)^2},(1/2-\epsilon)^2\right\}
\right].
\]

1bade2c33142628f4ff3614af5e53c22a7baad4c.title: Selected estimator \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\)
1bade2c33142628f4ff3614af5e53c22a7baad4c.body:
For fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) and fixed-interior upper-bound constant \(C_\epsilon\), define \(\widehat\tau^{\mathrm{sel}}_{C_\epsilon,\epsilon}\) to be \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\) when
\[
C_\epsilon\{1/n+d^2/(n^2\log^2 n)\}\le 1/n+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2,
\]
and to be \(\widehat\tau_{\mathrm{ctr}}\) otherwise.
